sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Outlook Calendar connector** integrates with Microsoft Outlook Calendar via the Microsoft Graph API. It enables Shielva to sync calendar events, meetings, and scheduling data in real-time.

This connector uses OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with delegated permissions, allowing users to grant access to their calendar data without sharing credentials.

**Key capabilities:**
- List all calendars for the authenticated user
- Sync events from any calendar within a configurable time window
- Incremental sync using delta tokens (future)
- Stable document IDs via SHA-256 hashing for deduplication
- Automatic token refresh with exponential back-off retry

children.title: Connector Type
children.content: `outlook_calendar` — registered in ACP as **Outlook Calendar**.

children.title: Provider
children.content: Microsoft (Azure Active Directory / Microsoft Graph API v1.0)

children.title: Supported Operations
children.content:
| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `install()` | Validates credentials and stores config |
| `authorize()` | Returns OAuth 2.0 authorization URL |
| `health_check()` | Calls `/me` to verify token validity |
| `sync()` | Fetches events from `/me/calendarView` for the next 30 days |
| `list_calendars()` | Returns all calendars via `/me/calendars` |
| `list_events()` | Lists events from a specific calendar |
| `get_event()` | Fetches a single event by ID |

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content: The connector uses **OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Flow** via Microsoft Identity Platform.

children.title: Auth Type
children.content: `oauth2` — users are redirected to Microsoft's consent screen and grant delegated `Calendars.Read` + `offline_access` permissions.

children.title: OAuth Endpoints
children.content:
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Authorization URI** | `https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize` |
| **Token URI** | `https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/token` |
| **Scopes** | `https://graph.microsoft.com/Calendars.Read`, `offline_access` |

children.title: Install Fields
children.content:
| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| Application (client) ID | `client_id` | string | Yes | Azure AD app client ID |
| Client Secret | `client_secret` | password | Yes | Azure AD client secret value |
| Azure AD Tenant | `tenant_hint` | string | No | Tenant ID for single-tenant apps; blank = multi-tenant |
| Redirect URI | `redirect_uri` | string | No | Must match exactly what's registered in Azure Portal |

children.title: Token Refresh
children.content:
Access tokens expire after 1 hour. The connector stores the `refresh_token` (provided when `offline_access` scope is granted) and calls the token endpoint automatically before each API call when the access token is expired.

If the refresh token is also expired or revoked, the connector returns `OutlookCalendarAuthError` and the user must re-authorize.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content: All configuration is stored in the connector's `config` dict (encrypted at rest by Shielva vault).

children.title: Config Keys
children.content:
| Key | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `client_id` | Azure AD application (client) ID |
| `client_secret` | Azure AD client secret |
| `tenant_hint` | Optional tenant ID or domain (default: `common`) |
| `redirect_uri` | OAuth redirect URI |
| `access_token` | Current access token (set post-auth) |
| `refresh_token` | Refresh token for background token renewal |
| `token_expires_at` | Unix timestamp when access token expires |

children.title: Azure Portal Setup
children.content:
1. Register an application in **Azure Active Directory → App registrations**.
2. Note the **Application (client) ID** and **Directory (tenant) ID**.
3. Create a **client secret** under Certificates & secrets.
4. Add redirect URI: your Shielva instance's `/connectors/oauth/callback`.
5. Grant API permissions: **Microsoft Graph → Delegated → Calendars.Read** + **offline_access**.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All methods are implemented in `connector.py` and delegate HTTP calls to `OutlookCalendarHTTPClient` in `client/http_client.py`.

children.title: install()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def install(self) -> InstallResult`

Validates that `client_id` and `client_secret` are present in config. Does not make network calls — full validation happens at `authorize()` and `health_check()`.

**Returns:** `InstallResult(success=True, message='...', connector_type='outlook_calendar')`

**Raises:** `OutlookCalendarAuthError` if required fields are missing.

children.title: authorize()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def authorize(self) -> str`

Builds and returns the Microsoft OAuth 2.0 authorization URL. The user is redirected to this URL to grant consent.

The `tenant_hint` config value is used as the tenant segment in the URL (defaults to `common` for multi-tenant / personal accounts).

**Returns:** Authorization URL string.

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def health_check(self) -> HealthCheckResult`

Calls `GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me` to verify the access token. Returns the authenticated user's display name and email.

**Returns:** `HealthCheckResult(healthy=True, message='Authenticated as <name>', details={...})`

**Raises:** `OutlookCalendarAuthError` on 401, `OutlookCalendarNetworkError` on connectivity issues.

children.title: sync()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def sync(self, **kwargs) -> SyncResult`

Fetches calendar events from `/me/calendarView` for the next 30 days. Handles `@odata.nextLink` pagination automatically.

**Query parameters:**
- `startDateTime`: current UTC time
- `endDateTime`: 30 days from now
- `$select`: subject, start, end, location, organizer, attendees, body, isAllDay, isCancelled, onlineMeeting
- `$top`: 100

**Returns:** `SyncResult` with list of `ConnectorDocument` objects and sync metadata.

Each document has a stable `id` derived from `SHA-256(calendar_id + event_id)`.

children.title: list_calendars()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_calendars(self) -> list[dict]`

Calls `GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/calendars` and returns all calendars for the user.

**Returns:** List of calendar dicts with `id`, `name`, `color`, `isDefaultCalendar`, `canEdit`.

children.title: list_events()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def list_events(self, calendar_id: str = 'primary', start: str = None, end: str = None) -> list[dict]`

Fetches events from a specific calendar using `calendarView` with the given start/end window (defaults to next 30 days).

**Returns:** List of raw Graph API event objects.

children.title: get_event()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def get_event(self, event_id: str, calendar_id: str = None) -> dict`

Fetches a single event by ID via `GET /me/events/{event_id}` or `GET /me/calendars/{calendar_id}/events/{event_id}`.

**Returns:** Raw Graph API event object.

**Raises:** `OutlookCalendarNotFoundError` if event does not exist (404).

sections.title: HTTP Client
sections.content: All HTTP calls are encapsulated in `OutlookCalendarHTTPClient` (`client/http_client.py`). The connector.py never calls `aiohttp` or `requests` directly.

children.title: Methods
children.content:
| Method | Graph API Call | Description |
|--------|---------------|-------------|
| `get_me()` | `GET /me` | Fetch authenticated user profile |
| `get_calendars()` | `GET /me/calendars` | List all user calendars |
| `get_events(calendar_id, start, end)` | `GET /me/calendarView` | Fetch events in date range, follows `@odata.nextLink` |
| `get_event(event_id)` | `GET /me/events/{id}` | Single event by ID |
| `post_form_data(url, data)` | POST with form encoding | Used for OAuth token exchange |

children.title: Error Handling
children.content:
Every response passes through `_raise_for_status()` which maps HTTP status codes to typed exceptions:

| HTTP Status | Exception |
|-------------|----------|
| 401 | `OutlookCalendarAuthError` |
| 403 | `OutlookCalendarAuthError` |
| 404 | `OutlookCalendarNotFoundError` |
| 429 | `OutlookCalendarRateLimitError` |
| 5xx | `OutlookCalendarNetworkError` |
| Other 4xx | `OutlookCalendarError` |

children.title: Retry Logic
children.content:
`with_retry()` in `helpers/utils.py` wraps API calls with exponential back-off:
- Max 3 attempts
- Back-off: 1s, 2s, 4s
- Skips retry on auth errors (`OutlookCalendarAuthError`) — re-authorizing is required
- Retries on network errors, rate limits, and 5xx responses

children.title: ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "id": "<sha256(calendar_id + event_id)>",
  "source": "outlook_calendar",
  "type": "calendar_event",
  "title": "<event subject>",
  "content": "<body preview>",
  "metadata": {
    "event_id": "<graph event ID>",
    "calendar_id": "<calendar ID>",
    "start": "2026-06-19T10:00:00",
    "end": "2026-06-19T11:00:00",
    "timezone": "UTC",
    "is_all_day": false,
    "is_cancelled": false,
    "location": "<location>",
    "organizer": "organizer@example.com",
    "attendees": ["attendee@example.com"],
    "online_meeting_url": "https://teams.microsoft.com/..."
  },
  "synced_at": "2026-06-19T00:00:00Z"
}
```

children.title: Document ID Stability
children.content: The `id` field is computed as `SHA-256(calendar_id + ':' + event_id)` truncated to 16 hex characters. This is stable across syncs — the same event always produces the same Shielva document ID, enabling upsert deduplication.

sections.title: Exception Hierarchy
sections.content:
All exceptions inherit from `OutlookCalendarError` (base) in `exceptions.py`:

```
OutlookCalendarError (base)
├── OutlookCalendarAuthError      — 401/403, token expired, missing credentials
├── OutlookCalendarNetworkError   — connectivity failures, 5xx
├── OutlookCalendarNotFoundError  — 404, resource not found
└── OutlookCalendarRateLimitError — 429, Graph API throttling
```

Callers can catch the base `OutlookCalendarError` or specific subclasses for finer control.

children.title: Common Errors
children.content:
| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| `401 Unauthorized` | Access token expired | Re-authorize the connector in ACP |
| `403 Forbidden` | `Calendars.Read` permission not granted | Add permission in Azure Portal → API permissions |
| `invalid_client` on token exchange | Wrong `client_id` or `client_secret` | Verify credentials in Azure Portal |
| `redirect_uri_mismatch` | Redirect URI mismatch | Ensure Redirect URI in ACP matches Azure Portal exactly |
| `429 Too Many Requests` | Graph API rate limit | Connector retries automatically with exponential back-off |
| Events not appearing | Sync window is next 30 days only | Events outside this window are not fetched in the current sync |

children.title: Testing
children.content:
Run the full test suite (64 tests, all mocked — no live credentials needed):

```bash
cd /Users/vivekvarshavaishvik/Documents/client_dir/outlook_calendar_connector
source "/Volumes/V3-SSD/Shielva Project Dirs/.venv/bin/activate"
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

All 64 tests must pass before registering the connector.
